other_observers: My two sons, Ty and Ben Mark Peterson arrived about 10 minutes after discovery; I was the original finder and indetifier
description: Overall size: thrush sized, same as American Robin  First seen in flight - diagnostic orange fringing wing pattern clearly observed and first indicator of species id.  Perched - broad orange supercillium bordered by bluish gray cap and black face stripe; black breast band across orange breast and throat; orange wing bars; bluish-gray back, cap and upper tail.Behaviors: Behavior: first flew in and perched at eye level in response to pishing; then, when being pursued for better looks, it perched in the tops of taller trees and acted rather shy. Eventually flew several hundred yards to private property - thrush like flight.Call: quiet "thuck" while perched high in tree, repeated several timesPlumage: adult
